title: Listening - Part 4: Career Changes to Farming
description: You will hear five short extracts in which people talk about leaving corporate jobs to start organic farms. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I used to spend ten hours a day in a windowless office, managing hedge funds. [21]The constant digital noise and the lack of a tangible product just became soul-crushing[/21]. I needed to touch the earth. Now, my days start at 5 AM, and while I’m physically exhausted, [26]the sense of satisfaction when I see a field of healthy crops is something I never found in finance[/26]. It’s about producing something real. || Speaker 2: My friends thought I was having a mid-life crisis when I quit my law firm. Truthfully, [22]I was just tired of the ethical compromises and the endless paperwork[/22]. I wanted a simpler life for my kids. The transition wasn't easy, especially financially, but [27]the support from the local farming community has been a revelation[/27]. I thought I’d be isolated, but I’ve found a network of people who actually look out for one another. || Speaker 3: I was a software engineer in Silicon Valley, and while the money was great, [23]I felt completely disconnected from the natural world[/23]. I was building apps that didn't really matter. Moving to a small-scale poultry farm was a shock to the system. [28]What I appreciate most now is the way my schedule is dictated by the seasons[/28], not by artificial deadlines or quarterly targets. It’s a much more human way to live. || Speaker 4: People ask me if I miss the excitement of the fashion industry. Not for a second. [24]The waste and the superficiality of that world started to keep me up at night[/24]. I wanted to do something that contributed to the health of the planet. Starting an organic vineyard was a massive gamble, but [29]developing a deep understanding of soil chemistry has been intellectually fascinating[/29]. It’s much more scientific and demanding than people realize. || Speaker 5: I was a marketing executive for a major supermarket chain. [25]I saw how much food was being rejected for purely aesthetic reasons, and it broke my heart[/25]. I decided I wanted to grow food the right way. My farm is small, but we use heritage seeds. [30]I love the direct contact I have with my customers at the weekly markets[/30]. Hearing how much they enjoy the flavour of my vegetables makes every back-breaking hour in the mud worth it.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) why each speaker decided to leave their previous job.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 felt the lack of a 'tangible product' and the digital environment were 'soul-crushing'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 was tired of 'ethical compromises' and the nature of legal paperwork.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 felt 'completely disconnected from the natural world'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 was concerned about the 'waste and superficiality' of the fashion industry.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 was upset by the food waste caused by aesthetic standards in supermarkets.

options.A: a dislike of wasteful industry standards
options.B: a desire to reconnect with nature
options.C: the need for a higher income
options.D: moral concerns about their field of work
options.E: the lack of a physical result in their work
options.H: a sudden redundancy from their firm

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker finds most rewarding about their new life.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 finds satisfaction in seeing the 'tangible' results of healthy crops.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 values the unexpected 'support from the local farming community'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 enjoys having a schedule 'dictated by the seasons' rather than deadlines.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 finds the 'scientific' study of soil chemistry intellectually stimulating.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 values the 'direct contact' and feedback from customers at markets.

options.A: the financial freedom they have gained
options.B: the intellectual challenge of the work
options.C: the visible success of their production
options.D: the quiet isolation of the countryside
options.E: the improvement in their physical fitness
options.F: the natural rhythm of the working day
options.H: the sense of belonging to a group
